heading: Use of certain gifts
label: 7456 Use of certain gifts
text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the Army, the Superintendent of the Academy may (without regard to ) accept, hold, administer, invest, and spend any gift, devise, or bequest of personal property of a value of $20,000 or less made to the United States on the condition that such gift, devise, or bequest be used for the benefit of the Academy or any entity thereof. The Secretary may pay or authorize the payment of all reasonable and necessary expenses in connection with the conveyance or transfer of a gift, devise, or bequest under this section.section 2601 of this title

L. 97–29596 Stat. 1297Oct. 12, 1982“(b)This section applies with respect to any gift, devise, or bequest made on or after the date of the enactment of this Act [] for the purpose described in subsection (a) [see text of ] and applies to any such gift, devise, or bequest, or devise made before the date of the enactment of this Act with respect to which the Secretary of the Army has approved application of this section rather than , United States Code.”Sept. 8, 1982section 7456 of this titlesection 2601 of title 10
